Luxuria est emere hōs, quis enim dubitatve negatve?
sed multō major vēndere luxuria est.

author: Martial
text:
Hystericam vetulō sē dīxerat esse marītō
et queritur futuī Lēda necesse sibī;
sed flēns atque gemēns tantī negat esse salūtem
sēque refert potius prōposuisse morī.
Vir rogat ut vīvat viridēs nec dēserat annōs,
et fierī quod jam nōn facit ipse sinit.
Prōtinus accēdunt medicī medicaeque recēdunt,
tollunturque pedēs. Ō medicīna gravis!

author: Martial
text:
Draucī Natta suī vorat pipinnam,
collātus cui gallus est Priāpus.

author: Martial
text:
Ventūrum jūrās semper mihi, Lygde, rogantī
cōnstituisque hōram cōnstituisque locum.
Cum frūstrā jacuī longā prūrīgine tentus,
succurrit prō tē saepe sinistra mihī.
Quid precer, ō fallāx, meritīs et mōribus istīs?
Umbellam luscae, Lygde, ferās dominae.

author: Martial
text:
Cūrandum pēnem commīsit Baccara Raetus
rīvālī medicō. Baccara Gallus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Thēcā tēctus ahēneā lavātur
tēcum, Caelia, servus; ut quid, ōrō,
nōn sit cum citharoedus aut choraulēs?
Nōn vīs, ut puto, mentulam vidēre.
Quārē cum populō lavāris ergō?
Omnēs an tibi nōs sumus spadōnēs?
Ergō, nē videāris invidēre,
servō, Caelia, fībulam remitte.

author: Martial
text:
Solvere, Paete, decem tibi mē sēstertia cōgis,
perdiderit quoniam Bucco ducenta tibī.
Nē noceant, ōrō, mihi nōn mea crīmina: tū quī
bis centēna potes perdere, perde decem.

author: Martial
text:
In omnibus Vacerra quod conclāvibus
cōnsūmit hōrās et diē tōtō sedet,
cēnāturit Vacerra, nōn cacāturit.

author: Martial
text:
Ūtere fēmineīs complexibus, ūtere, Victor,
ignōtumque sibī mentula discat opus.
Flammea texuntur spōnsae, jam virgo parātur,
tondēbit puerōs jam nova nūpta tuōs.
Pēdīcāre semel cupidō dabit illa marītō,
dum metuit tēlī vulnera prīma novī:
saepius hoc fierī nūtrīx māterque vetābunt
et dīcent: “uxor, nōn puer, ista tibi est."
Heu quantōs aestūs, quantōs patiēre labōrēs,
sī fuerit cunnus rēs peregrīna tibī!
Ergo Subūrānae tīrōnem trāde magistrae.
Illa virum faciet; nōn bene virgo docet.

author: Martial
text:
Ad prīmum decimā lapidem quod vēnimus hōrā,
arguimur lentae crīmine pigritiae.
Nōn est ista viae, nōn est mea, sed tua culpa est,
mīsistī mūlās quī mihi, Paete, tuās.

author: Martial
text:
Lītus beātae Veneris aureum Bājās,
Bājās superbae blanda dōna Nātūrae,
ut mīlle laudem, Flacce, versibus Bājās,
laudābo dignē nōn satis tamen Bājās.
Sed Mārtiālem mālo, Flacce, quam Bājās.
Optāre utrumque pariter improbī vōtum est.
Quod sī deōrum mūnere hoc mihī dētur,
quid gaudiōrum est Mārtiālis et Bājae!

author: Martial
text:
Cum sene commūnem vexat spado Dindymus Aeglēn
et jacet in mediō sicca puella torō.
Vīribus hic, operī nōn est hic ūtilis annīs:
ergo sine effectū prūrit utrīque labor.
Supplex illa rogat prō sē miserīsque duōbus,
hunc juvenem faciās, hunc, Cytherēa, virum.

author: Martial
text:
Ā Sinuessānīs convīva Philostratus undīs
conductum repetēns nocte jubente larem
paene imitātus obīt saevīs Elpēnora fātīs,
praeceps per longōs dum ruit usque gradūs.
Nōn esset, Nymphae, tam magna perīcula passus
sī potius vestrās ille bibisset aquās.

author: Martial
text:
Nēmo habitat grātīs nisi dīves et orbus apud tē.
Nēmo domum plūris, Sōsibiāne, locat.

author: Martial
text:
Quī nōndum Stygiās dēscendere quaerit ad umbrās
tōnsōrem fugiat, sī sapit, Antiochum.
Alba minus saevīs lacerantur bracchia cultrīs,
cum furit ad Phrygiōs enthea turba modōs;
mītior implicitās Alcōn secat enterocēlās
frāctaque fabrīlī dēdolat ossa manū.
Tondeat hic inopēs Cynicōs et Stōica menta
collaque pulvereā nūdet equīna jubā.
Hic miserum Scythicā sub rūpe Promēthea rādat,
carnificem nūdō pectore poscet avem;
ad mātrem fugiet Pentheus, ad Maenadas Orpheus,
Antiochī tantum barbara tēla sonent.
Haec quaecumque meō numerātis stigmata mentō,
in vetulī pyctae quālia fronte sedent,
nōn īrācundīs fēcit gravis unguibus uxor:
Antiochī ferrum est et scelerāta manus.
Ūnus dē cūnctīs animālibus hircus habet cor:
barbātus vīvit, nē ferat Antiochum.

author: Martial
text:
Sīdere percussa est subitō tibi, Zōile, lingua,
dum lingis. Certē, Zōile, nunc futuis.

author: Martial
text:
Lēniat ut faucēs medicus, quās aspera vexat
assiduē tussis, Parthenopaee, tibī,
mella darī nucleōsque jubet dulcēsque placentās
et quidquid puerōs nōn sinit esse trucēs.
At tū nōn cessās tōtīs tussīre diēbus.
Nōn est haec tussis, Parthenopaee, gula est.

author: Martial
text:
Dīves erās quondam: sed tunc pēdīco fuistī
et tibi nūlla diū fēmina nōta fuit.
Nunc sectāris anūs. Ō quantum cōgit egestās!
Illa futūtōrem tē, Charidēme, facit.

author: Martial
text:
Multīs jam, Lupe, posse sē diēbus
pēdīcāre negat Carīsiānus.
Causam cum modo quaererent sodālēs,
ventrem dīxit habēre sē solūtum.

author: Martial
text:
Intāctās quārē mittis mihi, Polla, corōnās?
Ā tē vexātās mālo tenēre rosās.

author: Martial
text:
Carmina nūlla probās mollī quae līmite currunt,
sed quae per salebrās altaque saxa cadunt,
et tibi Maeoniō quoque carmine majus habētur,
“Lūcīlī columella hīc situs Metrophanēs";
attonitusque legis “terrāī frūgiferāī",
Accius et quidquid Pācuviusque vomunt.
Vīs imiter veterēs, Chrestille, tuōsque poētās?
Dispeream nī scīs mentula quid sapiat.

author: Martial
text:
Aeolidos Canacē jacet hōc tumulāta sepulchrō,
ultima cui parvae septima vēnit hiems.
"Āh scelus, āh facinus!" Properās quī flēre, viātor,
nōn licet hīc vītae dē brevitāte querī:
trīstius est lētō lētī genus: horrida vultūs
abstulit et tenerō sēdit in ōre luēs,
ipsaque crūdēlēs ēdērunt ōscula morbī
nec data sunt nigrīs tōta labella rogīs.
Sī tam praecipitī fuerant ventūra volātū,
dēbuerant aliā fāta venīre viā.
Sed mors vōcis iter properāvit clūdere blandae,
nē posset dūrās flectere lingua deās.

author: Martial
text:
Mentītur quī tē vitiōsum, Zōile, dīcit.
Nōn vitiōsus homō es, Zōile, sed vitium.

author: Martial
text:
Pīeriōs vātis Theodōrī flamma penātēs
abstulit. Hoc Mūsīs et tibi, Phoebe, placet?
Ō scelus, ō magnum facinus crīmenque deōrum,
nōn ārsit pariter quod domus et dominus!

author: Martial
text:
Quod nimium līvēs nostrīs et ubīque libellīs
dētrahis, ignōscō: verpe poēta, sapis.
Hoc quoque nōn cūrō, quod cum mea carmina carpās,
compīlās: et sīc, verpe poēta, sapis.
Illud mē cruciat, Solymīs quod nātus in ipsīs
pēdīcās puerum, verpe poēta, meum.
Ecce negās jūrāsque mihī per templa Tonantis.
Nōn crēdō: jūrā, verpe, per Anchialum.

author: Martial
text:
Inciderīs quotiēns in bāsia fellātōrum,
in solium puta tē mergere, Flacce, caput.

author: Martial
text:
Marcia, nōn Rhēnus, salit hīc, Germāne: quid obstās
et puerum prohibēs dīvitis imbre lacūs?
Barbare, nōn dēbet, summōtō cīve ministrō,
captīvam victrīx unda levāre sitim.

author: Martial
text:
Ūnā nocte quater possum: sed quattuor annīs
sī possum, peream, tē Telesilla semel.

author: Martial
text:
Effugere nōn est, Flacce, bāsiātōrēs.
Īnstant, morantur; persecuntur, occurrunt
et hinc et illinc, usquequāque, quācumque.
Nōn ulcus ācre pūsculaeve lūcentēs,
nec trīste mentum sordidīque līchēnēs,
nec lābra pinguī dēlibūta cērātō,
nec congelātī gutta prōderit nāsī.
Et aestuantem bāsiant et algentem,
et nūptiāle bāsium reservantem.
Nōn tē cucullīs asseret caput tēctum,
lectīca nec tē tūta pelle vēlōque,
nec vindicābit sella saepibus clūsa:
rīmās per omnīs bāsiātor intrābit.
Nōn cōnsulātus ipse, nōn tribūnātus
sēnīve fascēs nec superba clāmōsī
līctōris abiget virga bāsiātōrem:
sedeās in altō tū licet tribūnālī
et ē curūlī jūra gentibus reddās,
ascendet illa bāsiātor atque illa.
Febrīcitantem bāsiābit et flentem,
dabit ōscitantī bāsium natantīque,
dabit cacantī. Remedium malī sōlum est,
faciās amīcum bāsiāre quem nōlīs.

author: Martial
text:
Dē cathedrā quotiēns surgis, jam saepe notāvī,
pēdīcant miserae, Lesbia, tē tunicae.
Quās cum cōnāta es dextrā, cōnāta sinistrā
vellere, cum lacrimīs eximis et gemitū:
sīc cōnstringuntur geminā Symplēgade cūlī
et nimiās intrant Cȳaneāsque natīs.
Ēmendāre cupis vitium dēfōrme? docēbō:
Lesbia, nec surgās cēnseo nec sedeās.

author: Martial
text:
Habēre amīcam nōlo, Flacce, subtīlem,
cujus lacertōs ānulī meī cingant,
quae clūne nūdō rādat et genū pungat,
cui serra lumbīs, cuspis ēminet cūlō.
Sed īdem amīcam nōlo mīlle lībrārum.
Carnārius sum, pinguiārius nōn sum.

author: Martial
text:
Thāida tam tenuem potuistī, Flacce, vidēre?
Tū, puto, quod nōn est, Flacce, vidēre potes.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn est mentītus quī tē mihi dīxit habēre
fōrmōsam carnem, Lȳdia, nōn faciem.
Est ita, sī taceās et sī tam mūta recumbās
quam silet in cērā vultus et in tabulā.
Sed quotiēns loqueris, carnem quoque, Lȳdia, perdis
et sua plūs nūllī quam tibi lingua nocet.
Audiat aedīlis nē tē videatque cavētō:
portentum est, quotiēns coepit imāgo loquī.

author: Martial
text:
Tanta tibi est animī probitās ōrisque, Safrōnī,
ut mīrer fierī tē potuisse patrem.

author: Martial
text:
Uxor, vāde forās aut mōribus ūtere nostrīs:
nōn sum ego nec Curius nec Numa nec Tatius.
Mē jūcunda juvant tractae per pōcula noctēs:
tū properās pōtā surgere trīstis aquā.
Tū tenebrīs gaudēs: mē lūdere teste lucernā
et juvat admissā rumpere lūce latus.
Fascia tē tunicaeque obscūraque pallia cēlant:
at mihi nūlla satis nūda puella jacet.
bāsia mē capiunt blandās imitāta columbās:
tū mihi dās aviae quālia māne solēs.
Nec mōtū dignāris opus nec vōce juvāre
nec digitīs, tamquam tūra merumque parēs:
masturbābantur Phrygiī post ōstia servī,
Hectoreō quotiēns sēderat uxor equō,
et quamvīs Ithacō stertente pudīca solēbat
illīc Pēnelopē semper habēre manum.
Pēdīcāre negās: dabat hoc Cornēlia Gracchō,
Jūlia Pompejō, Porcia, Brūte, tibī;
dulcia Dardaniō nōndum miscente ministrō
pōcula Jūno fuit prō Ganymēde Jovī.
Sī tē dēlectat gravitās, Lūcrētia tōtō
sīs licet usque diē: Lāida nocte volō.

author: Martial
text:
Mittēbās lībram, quadrantem, Garrice, mittis.
Saltem sēmissem, Garrice, solve mihī.

author: Martial
text:
Vībī Maxime, sī vacās havēre,
hoc tantum lege: namque et occupātus
et nōn es nimium labōriōsus.
Trānsīs hōs quoque quattuor? Sapīstī.

author: Martial
text:
Explicitum nōbīs usque ad sua cornua librum
et quasi perlēctum, Septiciāne, refers.
Omnia lēgistī. Crēdō, scio, gaudeo, vērum est.
Perlēgī librōs sīc ego quīnque tuōs.

author: Martial
text:
Quamvīs tam longō possīs satur esse libellō,
lēctor, adhūc ā mē disticha pauca petis.
Sed Lupus ūsūram puerīque diāria poscunt.
Lēctor, solve. Tacēs dissimulāsque? Valē.

author: Martial
text:
Rētia dum cessant lātrātōrēsque Molossī
Et nōn inventō silva quiēscit aprō,
Ōtia, Prīsce, brevī poteris dōnāre libellō.
Hōra nec aestīva est nec tibi tōta perit.

author: Martial
text:
Ad populōs mittī quī nūper ab Urbe solēbās,
Ībis, iō, Rōmam nunc peregrīne liber
Auriferī dē gente Tagī tetricīque Salōnis,
Dat patriōs amnēs quōs mihi terra potēns.
Nōn tamen hospes eris, nec jam potes advena dīcī,
Cujus habet frātrēs tot domus alta Remī.
Jūre tuō veneranda novī pete līmina templī,
Reddita Pīeriō sunt ubi tēcta chorō.
Vel sī māluerīs, prīmā gradiēre Subūrā;
Ātria sunt illīc cōnsulis alta meī:
Laurigerōs habitat fācundus Stella penātīs,
Clārus Hyantēae Stēlla sitītor aquae;
Fōns ibi Castalius vitreō torrente superbit,
Unde novem dominās saepe bibisse ferunt:
Ille dabit populō patribusque equitīque legendum,
Nec nimium siccīs perleget ipse genīs.
Quid titulum poscis? versūs duo trēsve legantur,
Clāmābunt omnēs tē, liber, esse meum.

author: Martial
text:
Quod Flaccō Variōque fuit summōque Marōnī
Maecēnās, atavīs rēgibus ortus eques,
Gentibus et populīs, hoc tē mihi, Prīsce Terentī,
Fāma fuisse loquāx chartaque dīcet anus
Tū facis ingenium, tū, sī quid posse vidēmur;
Tū dās ingenuae jūs mihi pigritiae.
Macte animī, quem rārus habēs, mōrumque tuōrum,
Quōs Numa, quōs hilaris possit habēre Catō.
Largīrī, praestāre, brevēs extendere cēnsūs,
Et dare quae facilēs vix tribuēre deī,
Nunc licet et fās est. Sed tū sub prīncipe dūrō
Temporibusque malīs ausus es esse bonus.

author: Martial
text:
Longior ūndecimī nōbīs decimīque libellī
Artātus labor est et breve rāsit opus.
Plūra legant vacuī, quibus ōtia tūta dedistī:
Haec lege tū, Caesar; forsan et illa legēs.

author: Martial
text:
Quae modo lītoreōs ībātis carmina Pyrgōs,
Īte sacrā jam nōn pulverulenta viā.
Contigit Ausoniae procerum mītissimus aulae
Nerva: licet tōtō nunc Helicōne fruī:
Rēcta fidēs, hilaris clēmentia, cauta potestās
Jam redeunt; longī terga dedēre metūs.
Hoc populī gentēsque tuae, pia Rōma, precantur:
Dux tibi sit semper tālis, et iste diū.

author: Martial
text:
Tōtō vertice quot gerit capillōs,
Annōs sī tot habet Ligeia, trīma est.

author: Martial
text:
Terrārum dea gentiumque Rōma,
cui pār est nihil et nihil secundum,
Trājānī modo laeta cum futūrōs
Tot per saecula conputāret annōs,
Et fortem juvenemque Mārtiumque
In tantō duce mīlitem vidēret,
Dīxit praeside glōriōsa tālī:
‘Parthōrum procerēs ducēsque Sērum,
Thrācēs, Sauromatae, Getae, Britannī,
Possum ostendere Caesarem; venīte.'

author: Martial
text:
Palma regit nostrōs, mītissime Caesar, Hibērōs,
Et placidō fruitur pāx peregrīna jugō.
Ergō agimus laetī tantō prō mūnere grātēs:
Mīsistī mōrēs in loca nostra tuōs.

author: Martial
text:
Habet Āfricānus mīliēns, tamen captat.
Fortūna multīs dat nimis, satis nūllī.

author: Martial
text:
Partheniō dīc, Mūsa, tuō nostrōque salūtem:
Nam quis ab Āoniō largius amne bibit?
Cujus Piplēō lyra clārior exit ab antrō?
Quem plūs Pīeriō dē grege Phoebus amat?
Et sī forte (sed hoc vix est) spērāre vacābit,
Trādat ut ipse ducī carmina nostra, rogā,
Quattuor et tantum timidumque brevemque libellum
Commendet verbīs ‘Hunc tua Rōma legit.'

author: Martial
text:
Omnia prōmittis, cum tōtā nocte bibistī;
Māne nihil praestās. Pollio, māne bibe.

author: Martial
text:
Genus, Aucte, lūcrī dīvitēs habent īram:
Ōdisse, quam dōnāre, vīlius cōnstat.

author: Martial
text:
Parcius ūtāris, moneō, rapiente verēdō,
Prīsce, nec in leporēs tam violentus eās.
Saepe satisfēcit praedae vēnātor, et ācrī
Dēcidit excussus, nec reditūrus, equō.
Īnsidiās et campus habet: nec fossa nec agger
Nec sint saxa licet, fallere plāna solent.
Nōn dēerit quī tanta tibī spectācula praestet,
Invidiā fātī sed leviōre cadat.
Sī tē dēlectant animōsa perīcula, Tuscīs
Tūtior est virtūs īnsidiēmur aprīs.
Quid tē frēna juvant temerāria? saepius illīs,
Prīsce, datum est equitem rumpere, quam leporem.

author: Martial
text:
Quidquid Parrhasiā nitēbat aulā,
Dōnātum est oculīs deīsque nostrīs.
Mīrātur Scythicās virentis aurī
Flammās Juppiter, et stupet superbī
Rēgis dēliciās gravēsque lūsūs:
Haec sunt pōcula, quae decent Tonantem,
Haec sunt, quae Phrygium decent ministrum.
Omnēs cum Jove nunc sumus beātī;
At nūper pudet, ā pudet fatērī
Omnēs cum Jove pauperēs erāmus.

author: Martial
Martial
Martial
text:
Dum tū forsitan inquiētus errās
Clāmōsā, Juvenālis, in Subūrā,
Aut collem dominae teris Diānae;
Dum per līmina tē potentiōrum
Sūdātrīx toga ventilat vagumque
Major Caelius et minor fatīgant:
Mē multōs repetīta post Decembrēs
Accēpit mea rūsticumque fēcit
Aurō Bilbilis et superba ferrō.
Hīc pigrī colimus labōre dulcī
Bōterdum Plateamque (Celtibērīs
Haec sunt nōmina crassiōra terrīs):
Ingentī fruor inprobōque somnō,
Quem nec tertia saepe rumpit hōra,
Et tōtum mihi nunc repōno, quidquid
Ter dēnōs vigilāveram per annōs.
Ignōta est toga, sed datur petentī
Ruptā proxima vestis ā cathēdrā.
Surgentem focus excipit superbā
Vīcīnī strue cultus īlicētī,
Multā vīlica quem corōnat ōllā.
Vēnātor sequitur, sed ille quem tū
Sēcrētā cupiās habēre silvā;
Dispēnsat puerīs rogatque longōs
Lēvīs pōnere vīlicus capillōs.
Sīc mē vīvere, sīc juvat perīre.

author: Martial
text:
In thermīs sūmit lactūcās, ōva, lacertum,
Et cēnāre domī sē negat Aemilius.

author: Martial
text:
Quārē nōn habeat, Fabulle, quaeris
Uxōrem Themisōn? habet sorōrem.

author: Martial
text:
Mūnicipem rigidī quis tē, Marcella, Salōnis
Et genitam nostrīs quis putet esse locīs?
Tam rārum, tam dulce sapis. Pālātia dīcent,
Audierint sī tē vel semel, esse suam;
Nūlla nec in mediā certābit nāta Subūrā
Nec Capitōlīnī collis alumna tibī;
Nec cito rīdēbit peregrīnī glōria partūs,
Rōmānam deceat quam magis esse nurum.
Tū dēsīderium dominae mihi mītius urbis
Esse jubēs: Rōmam tū mihi sōla facis.

author: Martial
text:
Quam sit lusca Philaenis indecenter,
Vīs dīcam breviter tibī, Fabulle?
Esset caeca decentior Philaenis.

author: Martial
text:
Dentibus atque comīs (nec tē pudet) ūteris ēmptīs.
Quid faciēs oculō, Laelia? nōn emitur.

author: Martial
text:
Ō jūcunda, covinne, sōlitūdō,
Carrūcā magis essedōque grātum
Fācundī mihi mūnus Aeliānī!
Hīc mēcum licet, hīc, Juvāte, quidquid
In buccam tibi vēnerit, loquāris:
Nōn rēctor Libycī niger caballī,
Succīnctus neque cursor antecēdit;
Nusquam est mūlio: mannulī tacēbunt.
Ō sī cōnscius esset hīc Avītus,
Aurem nōn ego tertiam timērem.
Tōtus quam bene sīc diēs abīret!

author: Martial
text:
Cum rogo tē nummōs sine pignore, ‘nōn habeō' inquis;
Īdem, sī prō mē spondet agellus, habēs:
Quod mihi nōn crēdis veterī, Telesīne, sodālī,
Crēdis cōliculīs arboribusque meīs.
Ecce, reum Cārus tē dētulit: adsit agellus.
Exiliī comitem quaeris: agellus eat.

author: Martial
text:
Ā lātrōnibus esse tē futūtam
Dīcis, Saenia: sed negant latrōnēs.

author: Martial
text:
Pōtō ego sextantēs, tū pōtās, Cinna, deuncēs:
Et quereris quod nōn, Cinna, bibāmus idem?

author: Martial
text:
Hermogenēs tantus mappārum, Pontice, fūr est,
Quantus nummōrum vix, puto, Massa fuit;
Tū licet observēs dextram teneāsque sinistram,
Inveniet, mappam quā ratiōne trahat:
Cervīnus gelidum sorbet sīc hālitus anguem,
Cāsūrās altē sīc rapit Īris aquās.
Nūper cum Myrinō peterētur missio laesō,
Subdūxit mappās quattuor Hermogenēs;
Crētātam praetor cum vellet mittere mappam,
Praetōrī mappam surpuit Hermogenēs.
Attulerat mappam nēmō, dum fūrta timentur:
Mantēle ā mēnsā surpuit Hermogenēs.
Hoc quoque sī dēerit, mediōs discingere lectōs
Mēnsārumque pedēs nōn timet Hermogenēs.
Quamvīs nōn modicō caleant spectācula sōle,
Vēla redūcuntur, cum venit Hermogenēs.
Festīnant trepidī substringere carbasa nautae,
Ad portum quotiēns pāruit Hermogenēs.
Līnigerī fugiunt calvī sistrātaque turba,
Inter adōrantēs cum stetit Hermogenēs.
Ad cēnam Hermogenēs mappam nōn attulit umquam,
Ā cēnā semper rettulit Hermogenēs.

author: Martial
text:
Sexāgēna terās cum līmina māne senātor,
Esse tibī videor dēsidiōsus eques,
Quod nōn ā prīmā discurram lūce per urbem
Et referam lassus bāsia mīlle domum.
Sed tū, purpureīs ut dēs nova nōmina fāstīs
Aut Nomadum gentēs Cappadocumve petās:
At mihi, quem cōgis mediōs abrumpere somnōs
Et mātūtīnum ferre patīque lutum,
Quid petitur? Ruptā cum pēs vagus exit alūtā
Et subitus crassae dēcidit imber aquae
Nec venit ablātīs clāmātus verna lacernīs,
Accēdit gelidam servus ad auriculam
Et ‘Rogat ut sēcum cēnēs Laetōrius' inquit.
Vīgintī nummīs? nōn ego: mālo famem,
Quam sit cēna mihī, tibi sit prōvincia mercēs,
Et faciāmus idem nec mereāmur idem.

author: Martial
text:
Siccus, sōbrius est Aper; quid ad mē?
Servum sīc ego laudo, nōn amīcum.

author: Martial
text:
Hoc nemus, hī fontēs, haec textilis umbra supīnī
Palmitis, hoc riguae ductile flūmen aquae,
Prātaque nec biferō cessūra rosāria Paestō,
Quodque viret Jānī mēnse nec alget holus,
Quaeque natat clūsīs anguilla domestica lymphīs,
Quaeque gerit similēs candida turris avēs,
Mūnera sunt dominae: post septima lustra reversō
Hās Marcella domōs parvaque rēgna dedit.
Sī mihi Nausicaā patriōs concēderet hortōs,
Alcinoō possem dīcere ‘Mālo meōs.'

author: Martial
text:
Ō Jūliārum dēdecus Kalendārum,
Vīdī, Vacerra, sarcinās tuās, vīdī;
Quās nōn retentās pēnsiōne prō bīmā
Portābat uxor rūfa crīnibus septem
Et cum sorōre cāna māter ingentī.
Furiās putāvī nocte Dītis ēmersās.
Hās tū priōrēs frīgore et famē siccus
Et nōn recentī pallidus magis buxō
Īrus tuōrum temporum sequēbāris.
Migrāre clīvum crēderēs Arīcīnum.
Ībat tripēs grabātus et bipēs mēnsa,
Et cum lucernā corneōque crātēre
Matella curtō rupta latere mējēbat;
Focō virentī suberat amphorae cervīx;
Fuisse gerrēs aut inūtilēs maenās
Odor inpudīcus urceī fatēbātur,
Quālis marīnae vix sit aura piscīnae.
Nec quadra dēerat cāseī Tolōsātis,
Quadrīma nīgrī nec corōna pūlēī
Calvaeque restēs āliōque cēpīsque,
Nec plēna turpī mātris olla rēsīnā,
Summemmiānae quā pilantur uxōrēs
Quid quaeris aedēs vīlicēsque dērīdēs,
Habitāre grātīs, ō Vacerra, cum possīs?
Haec sarcinārum pompa convenit pontī.

author: Martial
text:
Ut puerōs emeret Labiēnus vēndidit hortōs.
Nīl nisi fīcētum nunc Labiēnus habet.

author: Martial
text:
Trīgintā mihi quattuorque messēs
Tēcum, sī meminī, fuēre, Jūlī.
Quārum dulcia mixta sunt amārīs,
Sed jūcunda tamen fuēre plūra;
Et sī calculus omnis hūc et illūc
Dīversus bicolorque dīgerātur,
Vincet candida turba nīgriōrem.
Sī vītāre velīs acerba quaedam
Et trīstīs animī cavēre morsūs,
Nūllī tē faciās nimis sodālem:
Gaudēbis minus et minus dolēbis.

author: Martial
text:
Tamquam simpliciter mēcum, Callistrate, vīvās,
Dīcere percīsum tē mihi saepe solēs.
Nōn es tam simplex, quam vīs, Callistrate, crēdī.
Nam quisquis nārrat tālia, plūra tacet.

author: Martial
text:
Lībrās quattuor aut duās amīcō
Algentemque togam brevemque laenam,
Interdum aureolōs manū crepantīs,
Possint dūcere quī duās Kalendās,
Quod nēmō, nisi tū, Labulle, dōnās,
Nōn es, crēde mihī, bonus. Quid ergō?
Ut vērum loquar, optimus malōrum es.
Pīsōnēs Senecāsque Memmiōsque
Et Crīspōs mihi redde, sed priōrēs:
Fīēs prōtinus ultimus bonōrum.
Vīs cursū pedibusque glōriārī?
Tigrim vince levemque Passerīnum:
Nūlla est glōria praeterīre asellōs.

author: Martial
text:
Nāsūtus nimium cupis vidērī.
Nāsūtum volo, nōlo pōlypōsum.

author: Martial
text:
Hunc quī fēmineīs noctēsque diēsque cathedrīs
Incēdit tōtā nōtus in urbe nimis,
Crīne nitēns, niger unguentō, perlūcidus ostrō,
Ōre tener, lātus pectore, crūre glaber,
Uxōrī quī saepe tuae comes inprobus haeret,
Nōn est quod timeās, Candide: nōn futuit.

author: Martial
text:
Odī tē, quia bellus es, Sabelle.
Rēs est pūtida bellus et Sabellus.
Bellum dēnique mālo, quam Sabellum.
Tābēscās utinam, Sabelle bellē!

author: Martial
text:
Mentīris, crēdō: recitās mala carmina, laudō:
Cantās, canto: bibis, Pontiliāne, bibō:
Pēdis, dissimulō: gemmā vīs lūdere, vincor:
Rēs ūna est, sine mē quam facis, et taceō.
Nīl tamen omnīnō praestās mihi. ‘Mortuus,' inquis,
‘Accipiam bene tē.' Nīl volo: sed morere.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn est, Tucca, satis, quod es gulōsus:
Et dīcī cupis et cupis vidērī.

author: Martial
text:
Barbātus rigidō nūpsit Callistratus Āfrō,
Hāc quā lēge virō nūbere virgo solet.
Praelūxēre facēs, vēlārunt flammea vultūs,
Nec tua dēfuerunt verba, Talasse, tibī.
Dōs etiam dicta est. Nōndum tibi, Rōma, vidētur
Hoc satis? expectās numquid ut et pariat?

author: Martial
text:
Fācundōs mihi dē libīdinōsīs
Lēgistī nimium, Sabelle, versūs,
Quālēs nec Didymī sciunt puellae
Nec mollēs Elephantidos libellī.
Sunt illīc Veneris novae figūrae,
Quālēs perditus audeat futūtor,
Praestent et taceant quid exolētī,
Quō symplēgmate quīnque cōpulentur,
Quā plūrēs teneantur ā catēnā,
Extīnctam liceat quid ad lucernam.
Tantī nōn erat, esse tē disertum.

author: Martial
text:
Ūnice, cognātō jūnctum mihi sanguine nōmen
Quī geris et studiō corda propinqua meīs;
Carmina cum faciās sōlī cēdentia frātrī,
Pectore nōn minor es, sed pietāte prior.
Lesbia cum lepidō tē posset amāre Catullō,
Tē post Nāsōnem blanda Corinna sequī.
Nec dēerant zephyrī, sī tē dare vēla juvāret;
Sed tū lītus amās. Hoc quoque frātris habēs.

author: Martial
text:
Haedīnā tibi pelle contegentī
Nūdae tempora verticemque calvae,
Fēstīvē tibi, Phoebe, dīxit ille,
Quī dīxit caput esse calceātum.

author: Martial
text:
Difficilis facilis, jūcundus acerbus es īdem:
Nec tēcum possum vīvere, nec sine tē.

author: Martial
text:
Vēndunt carmina Gallus et Lupercus.
Sānōs, Classice, nunc negā poētās.

author: Martial
text:
Bōlētōs et aprum sī tamquam vīlia pōnis
Et nōn esse putās haec mea vōta, volō:
Sī fortūnātum fierī mē crēdis et hērēs
Vīs scrībī propter quīnque Lucrīna, valē.
Lauta tamen cēna est: fateor, lautissima, sed crās
Nīl erit, immō hodiē, prōtinus immo nihil,
Quod sciat īnfēlīx damnātae spongia virgae
Vel quīcumque canis jūnctaque testa viae:
Mullōrum leporumque et sūminis exitus hic est,
Sulphureusque color carnificēsque pedēs.
Nōn Albāna mihī sit cōmissātio tantī
Nec Capitōlīnae pontificumque dapēs;
Inputet ipse deus nectar mihi, fīet acētum
Et Vātīcānī perfida vappa cadī.
Convīvās aliōs cēnārum quaere magister,
Quōs capiant mēnsae rēgna superba tuae:
Mē meus ad subitās invītet amīcus ofellās:
Haec mihi, quam possum reddere, cēna placet.

author: Martial
text:
Crīnītae Line paedagōge turbae,
Rērum quem dominum vocat suārum
Et crēdit cui Postumilla dīves
Gemmās, aurea, vīna, concubīnōs:
Sīc tē perpetuā fidē probātum
Nūllī nōn tua praeferat patrōna:
Succurrās miserō, precor, furōrī
Et servēs aliquando neglegenter
Illōs quī male cor meum perūrunt,
Quōs et noctibus et diēbus optō
In nostrō cupidus sinū vidēre,
Fōrmōsōs, niveōs, parēs, gemellōs,
Grandēs, nōn puerōs, sed ūniōnēs.

author: Martial
text:
Daphnōnas, platanōnas et āeriōs pityōnas
Et nōn ūnīus balnea sōlus habēs,
Et tibi centēnīs stat porticus alta columnīs,
Calcātusque tuō sub pede lūcet onyx,
Pulvereumque fugāx hippodromon ungula plaudit,
Et pereuntis aquae flūctus ubīque sonat;
Ātria longa patent. Sed nec cēnantibus usquam
Nec somnō locus est. Quam bene nōn habitās!

author: Martial
text:
Tam saepe nostrum dēcipī Fabullīnum,
Mīrāris, Aule? semper homo bonus tīrō est.

author: Martial
text:
Tempora Pīeriā solitus redimīre corōnā,
Nec minus attonitīs vōx celebrāta reīs,
Hīc situs est, hīc ille tuus, Semprōnia, Rūfus,
Cujus et ipse tuī flagrat amōre cinis.
Dulcis in Ēlysiō nārrāris fābula campō,
Et stupet ad raptūs Tyndaris ipsa tuōs:
Tū melior, quae dēsertō raptōre redistī,
Illa virum voluit nec repetīta sequī.
Rīdet et Īliacōs audit Menelāus amōrēs:
Absolvit Phrygium vestra rapīna Parim.
Accipient ōlim cum tē loca laeta piōrum,
Nōn erit in Stygiā nōtior umbra domō:
Nōn aliēna videt, sed amat Prōserpina raptās:
Iste tibī dominam conciliābit amor.

author: Martial
text:
Nummī cum tibi sint opēsque tantae,
Quantās cīvis habet, Paterne, rārus,
Largīris nihil incubāsque gāzae,
Ut magnus draco, quem canunt poētae
Cūstōdem Scythicī fuisse lūcī.
Sed causa, ut memorās et ipse jactās,
Dīrae fīlius est rapācitātis.
Ecquid tū fatuōs rudēsque quaeris,
Inlūdās quibus auferāsque mentem?
Huic semper vitiō pater fuistī.

author: Martial
text:
Crīne ruber, niger ōre, brevis pede, lūmine laesus,
Rem magnam praestās, Zōile, sī bonus es.

author: Martial
text:
Grātīs quī dare vōs jubet, puellae,
Īnsulsissimus inprobissimusque est.
Grātīs nē date, bāsiāte grātīs.
Hoc Aeglē negat, hoc avāra vēndit.
Sed vēndat: bene bāsiāre quantum est!
Hoc vēndit quoque nec levī rapīnā:
Aut lībram petit illa Cosmiānī,
Aut bīnōs quater ā novā monētā,
Nē sint bāsia mūta, nē maligna,
Nē clūsīs aditum neget labellīs.
Hūmānē tamen hoc facit, sed ūnum,
Grātīs quae dare bāsium recūsat
Grātīs lingere nōn recūsat, Aeglē.

author: Martial
text:
Aegrōtās ūnō deciēs aut saepius annō,
Nec tibi, sed nōbīs hoc, Polycharme, nocet:
Nam quotiēns surgis, sōtēria poscis amīcōs.
Sit pudor: aegrōtā jam, Polycharme, semel.

author: Martial
text:
Cūr saepe siccī parva rūra Nōmentī
Laremque vīllae sordidum petam, quaeris?
Nec cōgitandī, Sparse, nec quiēscendī
In urbe locus est pauperī. Negant vītam
Lūdī magistrī māne, nocte pistōrēs,
Aerāriōrum marculī diē tōtō;
Hinc ōtiōsus sordidam quatit mēnsam
Nerōniānā nummulārius massā,
Illinc balūcis malleātor Hispānae
Trītum nitentī fuste verberat saxum;
Nec turba cessat entheāta Bellōnae,
Nec fasciātō naufragus loquāx truncō,
Ā mātre doctus nec rogāre Jūdaeus,
Nec sulphurātae lippus īnstitor mercis.
Numerāre pīgrī damna quis potest somnī?
Dīcet quot aera verberent manūs urbis,
Cum secta Colchō Lūna vāpulat rhombō.
Tū, Sparse, nescīs ista, nec potēs scīre,
Petīliānīs dēlicātus in rēgnīs,
cui plāna summōs dēspicit domus montīs,
Et rūs in urbe est vīnitorque Rōmānus
Nec in Falernō colle major autumnus,
Intrāque līmen lātus essedō cursus,
Et in profundō somnus, et quiēs nūllīs
Offēnsa linguīs, nec diēs nisi admissus.
Nōs trānseuntis rīsus excitat turbae,
Et ad cubīle est Rōma. Taediō fessīs
Dormīre quotiēns libuit, īmus ad vīllam.

author: Martial
text:
Ancillāriolum tua tē vocat uxor, et ipsa
Lectīcāriola est: estis, Alauda, parēs.

author: Martial
text:
Tantum dat tibi Rōma bāsiōrum
Post annōs modo quīndecim reversō,
Quantum Lesbia nōn dedit Catullō.
Tē vīcīnia tōta, tē pilōsus
Hircōsō premit ōsculō colōnus;
Hinc īnstat tibi textor, inde fullō,
Hinc sūtor modo pelle bāsiātā,
Hinc mentī dominus perīculōsī,
Hinc et dexiochōlus, inde lippus,
Fellātorque recēnsque cunnilingus.
Jam tantī tibi nōn fuit redīre.

author: Martial
text:
Mārtis alumne diēs, roseam quō lampada prīmum
Magnaque sīdereī vīdimus ōra deī,
Sī tē rūre colī viridīsque pudēbit ad ārās,
Quī fuerās Latiā cultus in urbe mihī:
Dā veniam, servīre meīs quod nōlo Kalendīs,
Et quā sum genitus, vīvere lūce volō.
Nātālī pallēre suō, nē calda Sabellō
Dēsit; et ut liquidum pōtet Alauda merum,
Turbida sollicitō trānsmittere Caecuba saccō;
Atque inter mēnsās īre, redīre suās;
Excipere hōs illōs, et tōtā surgere cēnā
Marmora calcantem frīgidiōra gelū:
Quae ratiō est, haec sponte suā perferre patīque,
Quae tē sī jubeat rēx dominusque, negēs?

author: Martial
text:
Versūs et breve vīvidumque carmen
In tē nē faciam, timēs, Ligurra,
Et dignus cupis hōc metū vidērī.
Sed frūstrā metuis cupisque frūstrā.
In taurōs Libycī ruunt leōnēs,
Nōn sunt pāpiliōnibus molestī.
Quaerās, cēnseo, sī legī labōrās,
Nigrī fornicis ēbrium poētam,
Quī carbōne rudī putrīque crēta
Scrībit carmina, quae legunt cacantēs.
Frōns haec stigmate nōn meō notanda est.

author: Martial
text:
Antīquī rēx magne polī mundīque priōris,
Sub quō pigra quiēs nec labor ūllus erat,
Nec rēgāle nimis fulmen nec fulmine dignī,
Scissa nec ad Mānēs, sed sibi dīves humus:
Laetus ad haec facilisque venī sollemnia Prīscī
Gaudia: cum sacrīs tē decet esse tuīs.
Tū reducem patriae sextā, pater optime, brūmā
Pācificī Latiā reddis ab urbe Numae.
Cernis, ut Ausoniō similis tibi pompa macellō
Pendeat et quantus luxuriētur honōs?
Quam nōn parca manus largaeque nomismata mēnsae,
Quae, Sāturne, tibī pernumerentur opēs?
Utque sit hīs pretium meritīs et grātia major,
Et pater et frūgī sīc tua sacra colit.
At tū sāncte tuō sīc semper amēre Decembrī
Hōs illī jubeās saepe redīre diēs.

author: Martial
text:
Ūnctō Corduba laetior Venāfrō,
Histrā nec minus absolūta testā,
Albī quae superās ovēs Galaesī
Nūllō mūrice nec cruōre mendāx,
Sed tīnctīs gregibus colōre vīvō:
Dīc vestrō, rogo, sit pudor poētae,
Nec grātīs recitet meōs libellōs.
Ferrem, sī faceret bonus poēta,
cui possem dare mūtuōs dolōrēs.
Corrumpit sine tāliōne caelebs,
Caecus perdere nōn potest quod aufert:
Nīl est dēterius latrōne nūdō:
Nīl sēcūrius est malō poētā.

author: Martial
text:
Vincentem roseōs faciēque comāque ministrōs
Cinna cocum fēcit. Cinna, gulōsus homo es.

author: Martial
text:
Fōrmōsa Phyllis nocte cum mihī tōtā
Sē praestitisset omnibus modīs largam,
Et cōgitārem māne quod darem mūnus,
Utrumne Cosmī, Nīcerōtis an lībram,
An Baeticārum pondus ācre lānārum,
An dē monētā Caesaris decem flāvōs:
Amplexa collum bāsiōque tam longō
Blandīta, quam sunt nūptiae columbārum,
Rogāre coepit Phyllis amphoram vīnī.

author: Martial
text:
Bis quīnquāgēnīs domus est tibi mīlibus ēmpta,
Vēndere quam summā vel breviōre cupis.
Arte sed ēmptōrem vafrā corrumpis, Amoene,
Et casa dīvitiīs ambitiōsa latet.
Gemmantēs prīmā fulgent testūdine lectī,
Et Maurūsiacī pondera rāra citrī;
Argentum atque aurum nōn simplex Delphica portat;
Stant puerī, dominōs quōs precer esse meōs.
Deinde ducenta sonās, et ais nōn esse minōris.
Īnstrūctam vīlī vēndis, Amoene, domum.

author: Martial
text:
Mājae Mercurium creāstis Īdūs,
Augustīs redit Īdibus Diāna,
Octōbrēs Maro cōnsecrāvit Īdūs.
Īdūs saepe colās et hās et illās,
Quī magnī celebrās Marōnis Īdūs.

author: Martial
text:
Mātūtīne cliēns, urbis mihi causa relictae,
Ātria, sī sapiās, ambitiōsa colās.
Nōn sum ego causidicus, nec amārīs lītibus aptus,
Sed piger et senior Pīeridumque comes;
Ōtia mē somnusque juvant, quae magna negāvit
Rōma mihī: redeō, sī vigilātur et hīc.

author: Martial
text:
Sīc tanquam tabulās scyphōsque, Paule,
Omnēs archetypōs habēs amīcōs.

author: Martial
text:
Lintea ferret Aprō vatius cum vernula nūper
Et suprā togulam lusca sedēret anus
Atque oleī stillam daret enterocēlicus ūnctor,
Ūdōrum tetricus cēnsor et asper erat:
Frangendōs calicēs effundendumque Falernum
Clāmābat, biberet quod modo lōtus eques.
Ā sene sed postquam patruō vēnēre trecenta,
Sōbrius ā thermīs nescit abīre domum.
Ō quantum diatrēta valent et quīnque comātī!
Tunc, cum pauper erat, nōn sitiēbat Aper.

author: Martial
text:
Nīl nōn, Lygde, mihī negās rogantī:
At quondam mihi, Lygde, nīl negābās.

author: Martial
text:
Jūgera mercātus prope busta latentis agellī
Et male compactae culmina fulta casae,
Dēseris urbānās, tua praedia, Pannyche, lītēs
Parvaque, sed trītae praemia certa togae.
Frūmentum, milium tisanamque fabamque solēbās
Vēndere prāgmaticus, nunc emis agricola.

author: Martial
text:
Hērēdem tibi mē, Catulle, dīcis.
Nōn crēdam, nisi lēgerō, Catulle.

author: Martial
text:
Dum tibi Nīliacus portat crystalla cataplūs,
Accipe dē circō pōcula Flāminiō.
Hī magis audācēs, an sunt quī tālia mittunt
Mūnera? Sed gemmīs vīlibus ūsus inest:
Nūllum sollicitant haec, Flacce, toreumata fūrem,
Et nimium calidīs nōn vitiantur aquīs.
Quid, quod sēcūrō pōtat convīva ministrō,
Et cāsum tremulae nōn timuēre manūs?
Hoc quoque nōn nihil est, quod propīnābis in istīs,
Frangendus fuerit sī tibi, Flacce, calix.

author: Martial
Martial
text:
Amphora vīgēsīs, modius datur aere quaternō.
Ēbrius et crūdus, nīl habet agricola.

author: Martial
text:
Multīs dum precibus Jovem salūtat
Stāns summōs resupīnus usque in unguēs
Aethōn in Capitōliō pepēdit.
Rīsērunt hominēs, sed ipse dīvum
Offēnsus genitor, trinoctiālī
Adfēcit domicēniō clientem.
Post hoc flāgitium misellus Aethōn,
Cum vult in Capitōlium venīre,
Sellās ante petit Patercliānās
Et pēdit deciēsque vīciēsque.
Sed quamvīs sibi cāverit crepandō,
Compressīs natibus Jovem salūtat.

author: Martial
text:
Nīl in tē scrīpsī, Bīthȳnice. Crēdere nōn vīs
Et jūrāre jubēs? Mālo satis facere.

author: Martial
text:
Dōnāvī tibi multa quae rogāstī;
Dōnāvī tibi plūra quam rogāstī:
Nōn cessās tamen usque mē rogāre.
Quisquis nīl negat, Atticilla, fellat.

author: Martial
text:
Nē laudet dignōs, laudat Callistratus omnēs.
cui malus est nēmō, quis bonus esse potest?

author: Martial
Martial
text:
Effugere in thermīs et circā balnea nōn est
Mēnogenēn, omnī tū licet arte velīs.
Captābit tepidum dextrā laevāque trigōnem,
Inputet acceptās ut tibi saepe pilās.
Colliget et referet laxum dē pulvere follem,
Et sī jam lōtus, jam soleātus erit.
Lintea sī sūmēs, nive candidiōra loquētur,
Sint licet īnfantis sordidiōra sinū.
Exiguōs sectō cōmentem dente capillōs
Dīcet Achillēās disposuisse comās.
Fūmōsae feret ipse propīn dē faece lagōnae,
Frontis et ūmōrem colliget usque tuae.
Omnia laudābit, mīrābitur omnia, dōnec
Perpessus dīcās taedia mīlle ‘Venī!'

author: Martial
text:
Dērīsor Fabiānus hirneārum,
Omnēs quem modo cōleī timēbant
Dīcentem tumidās in hydrocēlas,
Quantum nec duo dīcerent Catullī,
In thermīs subitō Nerōniānīs
Vīdit sē miser, et tacēre coepit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōlueram, Polytīme, tuōs violāre capillōs,
Sed juvat hoc precibus mē tribuisse tuīs.
Tālis erās, modo tōnse Pelōps, positīsque nitēbās
Crīnibus, ut tōtum spōnsa vidēret ebur.

author: Martial
text:
Pēdīcōnibus ōs olēre dīcis.
Hoc sī, sīcut ais, Fabulle, vērum est:
Quid tū crēdis olēre cunnilingīs?

author: Martial
text:
Trīgintā tibi sunt puerī totidemque puellae:
Ūna est nec surgit mentula. Quid faciēs?

author: Martial
text:
Bis Cotta soleās perdidisse sē questus,
Dum neglegentem dūcit ad pedēs vernam,
Quī sōlus inopī praestat et facit turbam,
Excōgitāvit homo sagāx et astūtus
Nē facere posset tāle saepius damnum:
Excalceātus īre coepit ad cēnam.

author: Martial
text:
Tongiliānus habet nāsum: scio, nōn nego. Sed jam
Nīl praeter nāsum Tongiliānus habet.

author: Martial
text:
Quod lānā caput alligās, Charīne,
Nōn aurēs tibi, sed dolent capillī.

author: Martial
text:
Prō sene, sed clārē, vōtum Maro fēcit amīcō,
cui gravis et fervēns hēmitritaeos erat,
Sī Stygiās aeger nōn esset missus ad umbrās,
Ut caderet magnō victima grāta Jovī.
Coepērunt certam medicī spondēre salūtem.
Nē vōtum solvat, nunc Maro vōta facit.

author: Martial
text:
Commūnis tibi cum virō, Magulla,
Cum sit lectulus et sit exolētus,
Quārē, dīc mihi, nōn sit et minister.
Suspīrās; ratiō est, timēs lagōnam.

author: Martial
text:
Saepe rogāre solēs, quālis sim, Prīsce, futūrus,
Sī fīam locuplēs simque repente potēns.
Quemquam posse putās mōrēs nārrāre futūrōs?
Dīc mihi, sī fīās tū leo, quālis eris?

author: Martial
text:
Quā moechum ratiōne bāsiāret
Coram conjuge, repperit Fabulla.
Parvum bāsiat usque mōriōnem;
Hunc multīs rapit ōsculīs madentem
Moechus prōtinus, et suīs replētum
Rīdentī dominae statim remittit.
Quantō mōrio major est marītus!

author: Martial
text:
Scrībēbāmus epos; coepistī scrībere: cessī,
Aemula nē stārent carmina nostra tuīs.
Trānstulit ad tragicōs sē nostra Thalīa coturnōs:
Aptāstī longum tū quoque syrma tibī.
Fīla lyrae mōvī Calabrīs exculta Camēnīs:
Plēctra rapis nōbīs, ambitiōse, nova.
Audēmus saturās: Lūcīlius esse labōrās.
Lūdo levīs elegōs: tū quoque lūdis idem.
Quid minus esse potest? epigrammata fingere coepī:
Hinc etiam petitur jam mea palma tibī.
Ēlige, quid nōlīs quis enim pudor, omnia velle?
Et sī quid nōn vīs, Tucca, relinque mihī.

author: Martial
text:
Mussētī pathicissimōs libellōs,
Quī certant Sybarīticīs libellīs,
Et tīnctās sale prūriente chartās
Īnstantī lege Rūfe; sed puella
Sit tēcum tua, nē talassiōnem
Indīcās manibus libīdinōsīs
Et fīās sine fēminā marītus.

author: Martial
text:
Cum tibi nōta tuī sit vīta fidēsque marītī,
Nec premat ūlla tuōs sollicitetve torōs,
Quid quasi paelicibus torquēris inepta ministrīs,
In quibus et brevis est et fugitīva Venus?
Plūs tibi quam dominō puerōs praestāre probābō:
Hī faciunt, ut sīs fēmina sōla virō;
Hī dant quod nōn vīs uxor dare. ‘Dō tamen,' inquis,
‘Nē vagus ā thalamīs conjugis erret amor.'
Nōn eadem rēs est: Chīam volo, nōlo mariscam:
Nē dubitēs quae sit Chīa, marisca tua est.
Scīre suōs fīnēs mātrōna et fēmina dēbet:
Cēde suam puerīs, ūtere parte tuā.

author: Martial
text:
Uxor cum tibi sit puella, quālem
Vōtīs vix petat inprobus marītus,
Dīves, nōbilis, ērudīta, casta,
Rumpis, Basse, latus, sed in comātīs,
Uxōris tibi dōte quōs parāstī.
Et sīc ad dominam reversa languet
Multīs mentula mīlibus redēmpta;
Sed nec vōcibus excitāta blandīs,
Mollī pollice nec rogāta surgit.
Sit tandem pudor, aut eāmus in jūs.
Nōn est haec tua, Basse: vēndidistī.

author: Martial
text:
Baetis olīviferā crīnem redimīte corōnā,
Aurea quī nitidīs vellera tinguis aquīs;
Quem Bromius, quem Pallas amat; cui rēctor aquārum
Albula nāvigerum per freta pandit iter:
Ōminibus laetīs vestrās Īnstantius ōrās
Intret, et hic populīs ut prior annus eat.
Nōn ignōrat, onus quod sit succēdere Macrō:
Quī sua mētītur pondera, ferre potest

author: Martial
text:
Nē toga cordȳlīs et paenula dēsit olīvīs
aut inopem metuat sordida blatta famem,
perdite Nīliacās, Mūsae, mea damna, papȳrōs:
postulat ecce novōs ēbria brūma salēs.
Nōn mea magnanimō dēpugnat tessera tēlō
sēnio nec nostrum cum cane quassat ebur:
haec mihi charta nucēs, haec est mihi charta fritillus:
ālea nec damnum nec facit ista lucrum.

author: Martial
text:
Nāsūtus sīs usque licet, sīs dēnique nāsus,
quantum nōluerat ferre rogātus Atlāns,
et possīs ipsum tū dērīdēre Latīnum:
nōn potes in nūgās dīcere plūra meās
ipse ego quam dīxī. Quid dentem dente juvābit
rōdere? Carne opus est, sī satur esse velīs.
Nē perdās operam: quī sē mīrantur, in illōs
vīrus habē, nōs haec nōvimus esse nihil.
Nōn tamen hoc nimium nihil est, sī candidus aure
nec mātūtīnā sī mihi fronte venīs.

author: Martial
text:
Omnis in hōc gracilī XENIŌRUM turba libellō
cōnstābit nummīs quattuor ēmpta tibī.
Quattuor est nimium? Poterit cōnstāre duōbus,
et faciat lucrum bybliopōla Tryphōn.
Haec licet hospitibus prō mūnere disticha mittās,
sī tibi tam rārus quam mihi nummus erit.
Addita per titulōs sua nōmina rēbus habēbis:
praetereās, sī quid nōn facit ad stomachum.

author: Martial
text:
Sērus ut aetheriae Germānicus imperet aulae
utque diū terrīs dā pia tūra Jovī.

author: Martial
text:
Cērea quae patulō lūcet fīcēdula lumbō,
cum tibi sorte datur, sī sapis, adde piper.

author: Martial
text:
Nōs alicam, poterit mulsum tibi mittere dīves.
Sī tibi nōluerit mittere dīves, emēs.

author: Martial
text:
Sī spūmet rubrā conchis tibi pallida testā,
lautōrum cēnīs saepe negāre potes.

author: Martial
text:
Inbue plēbējās Clūsīnīs pultibus ollās,
ut satur in vacuīs dulcia musta bibās.

author: Martial
text:
Accipe Nīliacam, Pēlūsia mūnera, lentem:
vīlior est alicā, cārior illa fabā.

author: Martial
text:
Nec dōtēs simulae possīs numerāre nec ūsūs,
pistōrī totiēns cum sit et apta cocō.

author: Martial
text:
Mūlio quod nōn det tacitūrīs, accipe, mūlīs.
Haec ego cōpōnī, nōn tibi, dōna dedī.

author: Martial
text:
Tercentum Libycī modiōs dē messe colōnī
sūme, suburbānus nē moriātur ager.

author: Martial
text:
Ut sapiant fatuae, fabrōrum prandia, bētae,
ō quam saepe petet vīna piperque cocus!

author: Martial
text:
Clūdere quae cēnās lactūca solēbat avōrum,
dīc mihi, cūr nostrās inchoat illa dapēs?

author: Martial
text:
Sī vīcīna tibī Nōmentō rūra coluntur,
ad vīllam moneō, rūstice, ligna ferās.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi brūmālī gaudentia frīgore rāpa
quae damus, in caelō Rōmulus ēsse solet.

author: Martial
text:
Nē tibi pallentēs moveant fastīdia caulēs,
nitrātā viridis brassica fīat aquā.

author: Martial
text:
Fīla Tarentīnī graviter redolentia porrī
ēdistī quotiēns, ōscula clūsa datō.

author: Martial
text:
Mittit praecipuōs nemorālis Arīcia porrōs:
in niveō viridēs stīpite cerne comās.

author: Martial
text:
Hōs Amiternus ager fēlīcibus ēducat hortīs:
Nursīnās poteris parcius ēsse pilās.

author: Martial
text:
Mollis in aequoreā quae crēvit spīna Ravennā
nōn erit incultīs grātior asparagīs.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn habilis cyathīs et inūtilis ūva Lyaeō,
sed nōn pōtantī mē tibi nectar erō.

author: Martial
text:
Chīa senī similis Bacchō, quem Sētia mīsit,
ipsa merum sēcum portat et ipsa salem.

author: Martial
text:
Sī tibi Cecropiō saturāta Cydōnea melle
pōnentur, dīcās: "Haec melimēla placent".

author: Martial
text:
Pōma sumus Cybelēs: procul hinc discēde, viātor,
nē cadat in miserum nostra ruīna caput.

author: Martial
text:
Sorba sumus, mollēs nimium tendentia ventrēs:
aptius haec puerō quam tibi pōma dabis.

author: Martial
text:
Aurea porrigitur Jānī caryōta Kalendīs;
sed tamen hoc mūnus pauperis esse solet.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi quae tortā vēnērunt condita mētā,
sī majōra forent cottana, fīcus erat.

author: Martial
text:
Prūna peregrīnae cariē rūgōsa senectae
sūme: solent dūrī solvere ventris onus.

author: Martial
text:
Cāseus Etrūscae signātus imāgine Lūnae
praestābit puerīs prandia mīlle tuīs.

author: Martial
text:
Sī sine carne volēs jentācula sūmere frūgī,
haec tibi Vestīnō dē grege massa venit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn quemcumque focum nec fūmum cāseus omnem,
sed Vēlābrēnsem quī bibit, ille sapit.

author: Martial
text:
Trēbula nōs genuit; commendat grātia duplex,
sīve levī flammā sīve domāmur aquā.

author: Martial
text:
Cum sit anus conjūnx et sint tibi mortua membra,
nīl aliud bulbīs quam satur esse potes.

author: Martial
text:
Fīlia Pīcēnae veniō Lūcānica porcae:
pultibus hinc niveīs grāta corōna datur.

author: Martial
text:
Haec quae Pīcēnīs vēnit subducta trapētīs
inchoat atque eadem fīnit olīva dapēs.

author: Martial
text:
Aut Corcȳraeī sunt haec dē frondibus hortī,
aut haec Massȳlī pōma dracōnis erant.

author: Martial
text:
Subripuit pāstor quae nōndum stantibus haedīs
dē prīmō mātrum lacte colūstra damus.

author: Martial
text:
Lascīvum pecus et viridī nōn ūtile Bacchō
det poenās; nocuit jam tener ille deō.

author: Martial
text:
Candida sī croceōs circumfluit unda vitellōs,
Hesperius scombrī temperet ōva liquor.

author: Martial
text:
Lacte merō pāstum pigrae mihi mātris alumnum
pōnat, et Aetōlō dē sue dīves edat.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn tibi dē Libycīs tuburēs et apȳrina rāmīs,
dē Nōmentānīs sed damus arboribus.

author: Martial
text:
Lecta suburbānīs mittuntur apȳrina rāmīs
et vernae tuburēs. Quid tibi cum Libycīs?

author: Martial
text:
Ēsse putēs nōndum sūmen; sīc ūbere largō
et fluit et vīvō lacte papilla tumet.

author: Martial
text:
Sī Libycae nōbīs volucrēs et Phāsides essent,
acciperēs, at nunc accipe chortis avēs.

author: Martial
text:
Vīlia māternīs fuerāmus Persica rāmīs:
nunc in adoptīvīs Persica cāra sumus.

author: Martial
text:
Pīcentīna Cerēs niveō sīc nectare crēscit
ut levis acceptā spongea turget aquā.

author: Martial
text:
Argentum atque aurum facile est laenamque togamque
mittere; bōlētōs mittere difficile est.

author: Martial
text:
Cum mē fīcus alat, cum pāscar dulcibus ūvīs,
cūr potius nōmen nōn dedit ūva mihī?

author: Martial
text:
Rumpimus altrīcem tenerō quae vertice terram
tūbera, bōlētīs pōma secunda sumus.

author: Martial
text:
Texta rosīs fortasse tibī vel dīvite nardō,
at mihi dē turdīs facta corōna placet.

author: Martial
text:
Tōta quidem pōnātur anas, sed pectore tantum
et cervīce sapit: cētera redde cocō.

author: Martial
text:
Cum pinguis mihi turtur erit, lactūca valēbis;
et cocleās tibi habē. Perdere nōlo famem.

author: Martial
text:
Cerrētāna mihī fīat vel missa licēbit
dē Menapīs: lautī dē petasōne vorent.

author: Martial
text:
Musteus est: properā, cārōs nec differ amīcōs.
Nam mihi cum vetulō sit petasōne nihil.

author: Martial
text:
Tē fortasse magis capiat dē virgine porcā;
mē māterna gravī dē sue volva capit.

author: Martial
text:
Nīliacum rīdēbis holus lānāsque sequācēs,
inproba cum morsū fīla manūque trahēs.

author: Martial
text:
Aspice quam tumeat magnō jecur ānsere majus!
Mīrātus dīcēs: "Hoc, rogo, crēvit ubī"?

author: Martial
text:
Tōta mihī dormītur hiems et pinguior illō
tempore sum quō mē nīl nisi somnus alit.

author: Martial
text:
Gaudet in effossīs habitāre cunīculus antrīs.
Mōnstrāvit tacitās hostibus ille viās.

author: Martial
text:
Inter sapōrēs fertur ālitum prīmus
Īōnicārum gustus attagēnārum.

author: Martial
text:
Pāscitur et dulcī facilis gallīna farīnā,
pāscitur et tenebrīs. Ingeniōsa gula est.

author: Martial
text:
Nē nimis exhaustō macrēsceret inguine gallus,
āmīsit testēs. Nunc mihi gallus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Succumbit sterilī frūstrā gallīna marītō.
Hunc mātris Cybelēs esse decēbat avem.

author: Martial
text:
Pōnitur Ausoniīs avis haec rārissima mēnsīs:
hanc in piscīnā lūdere saepe solēs.

author: Martial
text:
Nē violēs tenerās perjūrō dente columbās,
trādita sī Gnidiae sunt tibi sacra deae.

author: Martial
text:
Inguina torquātī tardant hebetantque palumbī:
nōn edat hanc volucrem quī cupit esse salāx.

author: Martial
text:
Galbina dēcipitur calamīs et rētibus āles,
turget adhūc viridī cum rudis ūva merō.

author: Martial
text:
Pannonicās nōbīs numquam dedit Umbria cattās:
māvult haec dominō mittere dōna Pudēns.

author: Martial
text:
Mīrāris, quotiēns gemmantīs explicat ālās,
et potes hunc saevō trādere, dūre, cocō?

author: Martial
text:
Dat mihi pinna rubēns nōmen, sed lingua gulōsīs
nostra sapit. Quid sī garrula lingua foret?

author: Martial
text:
Argōā prīmum sum trānsportāta carīnā.
Ante mihī nōtum nīl nisi Phāsis erat.

author: Martial
text:
Ānsere Rōmānō quamvīs satur Hannibal esset,
ipse suās numquam barbarus ēdit avēs.

author: Martial
text:
Haec servāvit avis Tarpēī templa Tonantis.
Mīrāris? Nōndum fēcerat illa deus.

author: Martial
text:
Turbābis versūs nec littera tōta volābit,
ūnam perdiderīs sī Palamēdis avem.

author: Martial
text:
Rūstica sim an perdīx quid rēfert, sī sapor īdem est?
Cārior est perdīx. Sīc sapit illa magis.

author: Martial
text:
Dulcia dēfectā modulātur carmina linguā
cantātor cycnus fūneris ipse suī.

author: Martial
text:
Nōmen habet magnī volucris tam parva gigantis?
Et nōmen prasinī Porphyriōnis habet.

author: Martial
text:
Spīrat in advectō, sed jam piger, aequore mullus;
languēscit. Vīvum dā mare: fortis erit.

author: Martial
text:
Quae natat in Siculō grandis mūrēna profundō,
nōn valet exustam mergere sōle cutem.

author: Martial
text:
Quamvīs lāta gerat patella rhombum,
rhombus lātior est tamen patellā.

author: Martial
text:
Ēbria Bājānō vēnī modo concha Lucrīnō:
nōbile nunc sitiō luxuriōsa garum.

author: Martial
text:
Caeruleus nōs Līris amat, quem silva Marīcae
prōtegit: hinc squillae maxima turba sumus.

author: Martial
text:
Hic scarus, aequoreīs quī vēnit adēsus ab undīs,
vīsceribus bonus est, cētera vīle sapit.

author: Martial
text:
Prīnceps Nīliacī raperis, coracīne, macellī:
Pellaeae prior est glōria nūlla gulae.

author: Martial
text:
Iste licet digitōs testūdine pungat acūtā,
cortice dēpositā mollis echīnus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Sanguine dē nostrō tīnctās, ingrāte, lacernās
induis, et nōn est hoc satis, ēsca sumus.

author: Martial
text:
In Venetīs sint lauta licet convīvia terrīs,
prīncipium cēnae gōbius esse solet.

author: Martial
text:
Lāneus Euganeī lupus excipit ōra Timāvī,
aequoreō dulcēs cum sale pāstus aquās.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn omnis laudēs pretiumque aurāta merētur,
sed cui sōlus erit concha Lucrīna cibus.

author: Martial
text:
Ad Pālātīnās acipēnsem mittite mēnsās:
ambrosiās ōrnent mūnera rāra dapēs.

author: Martial
text:
Inter avēs turdus, sī quid mē jūdice certum est
inter quadripedēs mattea prīma lepus.

author: Martial
text:
Quī Diomēdēīs metuendus saetiger agrīs
Aetōlā cecidit cuspide, tālis erat.

author: Martial
text:
Dente timētur aper, dēfendunt cornua cervum:
inbellēs dammae quid nisi praeda sumus?

author: Martial
text:
Mātūtīnārum nōn ultima praeda ferārum
saevus oryx cōnstat quot mihi morte canum!

author: Martial
text:
Hic erat ille tuō domitus, Cyparisse, capistrō.
An magis iste tuus, Silvia, cervus erat?

author: Martial
text:
Dum tener est onager sōlāque lalīsio mātre
pāscitur, hoc īnfāns sed breve nōmen habet.

author: Martial
text:
Pendentem summā capream dē rūpe vidēbis:
cāsūram spērēs; dēspicit illa canēs.

author: Martial
text:
Dēlicium parvō dōnābis dorcada nātō:
jactātīs solet hanc mittere turba togīs.

author: Martial
text:
Pulcher adest onager: mittī vēnātio dēbet
dentis Erȳthraeī: jam removēte sinūs.

author: Martial
text:
Hoc tibi Campānī sūdāvit bāca Venāfrī:
unguentum quotiēns sūmis, et istud olet.

author: Martial
text:
Exspīrantis adhūc scombrī dē sanguine prīmō
accipe fastōsum, mūnera cāra, garum.

author: Martial
text:
Antipolītānī, fateor, sum fīlia thynnī:
essem sī scombrī, nōn tibi missa forem.

author: Martial
text:
Hoc tibi Thēsēī populātrīx mīsit Hymettī
Pallados ā silvīs nōbile nectar apis.

author: Martial
text:
Cum dederīs Siculōs mediae dē collibus Hyblae,
Cecropiōs dīcās tū licet esse favōs.

author: Martial
text:
Gnōsia Mīnōae genuit vīndēmia Crētae
hoc tibi, quod mulsum pauperis esse solet.

author: Martial
text:
Haec dē vītiferā vēnisse picāta Viennā
nē dubitēs, mīsit Rōmulus ipse mihī.

author: Martial
text:
Attica nectareum turbātis mella Falernum.
Miscērī decet hoc ā Ganymēde merum.

author: Martial
text:
Hoc dē Caesareīs mītis vīndēmia cellīs
mīsit, Iūlēō quae sibi monte placet.

author: Martial
text:
Surrentīna bibis? Nec murrina picta nec aurum
sūme: dabunt calicēs haec tibi vīna suōs.

author: Martial
text:
Dē Sinuessānīs vēnērunt Massica prēlīs:
condita quō quaeris cōnsule? Nūllus erat.

author: Martial
text:
Pendula Pomptīnōs quae spectat Sētia campōs,
exiguā vetulōs mīsit ab urbe cadōs.

author: Martial
text:
Haec Fundāna tulit fēlīx autumnus Opīmī.
Expressit mustum cōnsul et ipse bibit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn sum dē prīmō, fateor, trifolīna Lyaeō,
inter vīna tamen septima vītis erō.

author: Martial
text:
Caecuba Fundānīs generōsa cocuntur Amȳclīs,
vītis et in mediā nāta palūde viret.

author: Martial
text:
Pōtābis liquidum Signīna morantia ventrem?
Nē nimium sistās, sit tibi parca sitis.

author: Martial
text:
Amphora Nestoreā tibi Māmertīna senectā
sī dētur, quodvīs nōmen habēre potest.

author: Martial
text:
Tarraco, Campānō tantum cessūra Lyaeō
haec genuit Tuscīs aemula vīna cadīs.

author: Martial
text:
Nōmentāna meum tibi dat vīndēmia Bacchum:
sī tē Quīntus amat, commodiōra bibēs.

author: Martial
text:
Dē Spōlētīnīs quae sunt cariōsa lagōnīs
māluerīs quam sī musta Falerna bibās.

author: Martial
text:
Marsica Paelignī mittunt turbāta colōnī:
nōn tū, lībertus sed bibat illa tuus.

author: Martial
text:
Amphora Nīliacī nōn sit tibi vīlis acētī:
esset cum vīnum, vīlior illa fuit.

author: Martial
text:
Cum tua centēnōs expunget sportula cīvīs,
fūmea Massiliae pōnere vīna potes.

author: Martial
text:
Caerētāna Nepōs pōnat, Sētīna putābis.
Nōn pōnit turbae, cum tribus illa bibit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōbilis et lānīs et fēlīx vītibus Aulōn
det pretiōsa tibī vellera, vīna mihi.

author: Martial
text:
Unguentum hērēdī numquam nec vīna relinquās.
Ille habeat nummōs, haec tibi tōta datō.

author: Martial
text:
Dat festīnātās, Caesar, tibi brūma corōnās:
quondam vēris erat, nunc tua facta rosa est.

author: Martial
text:
Synthesibus dum gaudet eques dominusque senātor
Dumque decent nostrum pillea sūmpta Jovem;
Nec timet aedīlem mōtō spectāre fritillō,
Cum videat gelidōs tam prope verna lacūs:
Dīvitis alternās et pauperis accipe sortēs:
Praemia convīvae dent sua quisque suō.
‘Sunt apinae trīcaeque et sī quid vīlius istīs.'
Quis nescit? vel quis tam manifēsta negat?
Sed quid agam potius madidīs, Sāturne, diēbus,
Quōs tibi prō caelō fīlius ipse dedit?
Vīs scrībam Thēbās Trōjamve malāsve Mycēnās?
‘Lūde,' inquis, ‘nucibus'. Perdere nōlo nucēs.

author: Martial
text:
Quō vīs cumque locō potes hunc fīnīre libellum:
Versibus explicitum est omne duōbus opus.
Lemmata sī quaeris cūr sint adscrīpta, docēbō:
Ut, sī māluerīs, lemmata sōla legās.

author: Martial
text:
Secta nisi in tenuēs essēmus ligna tabellās,
Essēmus Libycī nōbile dentis onus.

author: Martial
text:
Caede juvencōrum dominī calet ārea fēlīx,
Quinquiplicī cērā cum datur altus honōs.

author: Martial
text:
Languida nē trīstēs obscūrent lūmina cērae,
Nigra tibī niveum littera pingat ebur.

author: Martial
text:
Tunc triplicēs nostrōs nōn vīlia dōna putābis,
Cum sē ventūram scrībet amīca tibī.

author: Martial
text:
Esse putā cērās, licet haec membrāna vocētur:
Dēlēbis, quotiēns scrīpta novāre volēs.

author: Martial
text:
Nōndum lēgerit hōs licet puella,
Nōvit quid cupiant Vitelliānī.

author: Martial
text:
Quod minimōs cernis, mittī nōs crēdis amīcae.
Falleris: et nummōs ista tabella rogat.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn est, mūnera quod putēs pusilla,
Cum dōnat vacuās poēta chartās.

author: Martial
text:
Seu leviter notō, seu cārō missa sodālī
Omnēs ista solet charta vocāre suōs.

author: Martial
text:
Hōs nisi dē flāvā loculōs implēre monētā
Nōn decet: argentum vīlia ligna ferant.

author: Martial
text:
Sī quid adhūc superest in nostrī faece locellī,
Mūnus erit. Nihil est: ipse locellus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Cum steterit nūllus vultū tibi tālus eōdem,
Mūnera mē dīcēs magna dedisse tibī.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn sim tālōrum numerō pār tessera, dum sit
Major, quam tālīs, ālea saepe mihī.

author: Martial
text:
Quae scit compositōs manus improba mittere tālōs,
Sī per mē mīsit, nīl nisi vōta feret.

author: Martial
text:
Hāc mihi bis sēnō numerātur tessera pūnctō;
Calculus hāc geminō discolor hoste perit.

author: Martial
text:
Īnsidiōsōrum sī lūdis bella latrōnum,
Gemmeus iste tibī mīles et hostis erit.

author: Martial
text:
Ālea parva nucēs et nōn damnōsa vidētur;
Saepe tamen puerīs abstulit illa natīs.

author: Martial
text:
Sortītus thēcam calamīs armāre mementō:
Cētera nōs dedimus, tū leviōra parā.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi erunt armāta suō graphiāria ferrō:
Sī puerō dōnēs, nōn leve mūnus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Lentiscum melius: sed sī tibi frondea cuspis
Dēfuerit, dentēs pinna levāre potest.

author: Martial
text:
Sī tibi mōrōsā prūrīgine verminat auris,
Arma damus tantīs apta libīdinibus.

author: Martial
text:
Splendida nē madidī violent bombȳcina crīnēs,
Fīgat acus tortās sustineatque comās.

author: Martial
text:
Quid faciet nūllōs hīc inventūra capillōs
Multifidō buxus quae tibi dente datur?

author: Martial
text:
Chattica Teutonicōs accendit spūma capillōs:
Captīvīs poteris cultior esse comīs.

author: Martial
text:
Sī mūtāre parās longaevōs cāna capillōs,
Accipe Mattiacās, quō tibi calva? pilās.

author: Martial
text:
Accipe quae nimiōs vincant umbrācula sōlēs:
Sit licet et ventus, tē tua vēla tegent.

author: Martial
text:
In Pompejānō tēcum spectābo theātrō:
Mandātus populō vēla negāre solet.

author: Martial
text:
Excipient aprōs expectābuntque leōnēs,
Intrābunt ursōs, sit modo firma manus.

author: Martial
text:
Sī dējecta gemās longō vēnābula rōstrō,
Hic brevis ingentem comminus ībit aprum.

author: Martial
text:
Mīlitiae decus hoc grātīque erit ōmen honōris,
Arma tribūnicium cingere digna latus.

author: Martial
text:
Pūgio, quem curvā signat brevis orbita vēnā.
Strīdentem gelidīs hunc Salo tīnxit aquīs.

author: Martial
text:
Pāx mē certa ducis placidōs curvāvit in ūsūs.
Agricolae nunc sum, mīlitis ante fuī.

author: Martial
text:
Cum fieret trīstis solvendīs auctio nummīs,
Haec quadringentīs mīlibus ēmpta fuit.

author: Martial
text:
Tondendīs haec arma tibī sunt apta capillīs;
Unguibus hic longīs ūtilis, illa genīs.

author: Martial
text:
Sēlēctōs nisi dās mihī libellōs,
Admittam tineās trucēsque blattās.

author: Martial
text:
Dat chartīs habilēs calamōs Memphītica tellūs;
Texantur reliquā tēcta palūde tibī.

author: Martial
text:
Dulcis cōnscia lectulī lucerna,
Quidquid vīs faciās licet, tacēbō.

author: Martial
text:
Ancillam tibi sors dedit lucernae,
Tōtās quae vigil exigit tenēbrās.

author: Martial
text:
Inlūstrem cum tōta meīs convīvia flammīs
Totque geram myxōs, ūna lucerna vocor.

author: Martial
text:
Hic tibi nocturnōs praestābit cēreus ignēs:
Subducta est puerō namque lucerna tuō.

author: Martial
text:
Nōmina candēlae nōbīs antīqua dedērunt:
Nōn nōrat parcōs ūncta lucerna patrēs.

author: Martial
text:
Esse vidēs lignum; servās nisi lūmina, fīet
Dē candēlābrō magna lucerna tibī.

author: Martial
text:
Haec quae difficilī turget pāgānica plūmā,
Folle minus laxa est et minus arta pilā.

author: Martial
text:
Sī mē nōbilibus scīs expulsāre sinistrīs,
Sum tua. Tū nescīs? rūstice, redde pilam.

author: Martial
text:
Īte procul, juvenēs: mītis mihi convenit aetās:
Folle decet puerōs lūdere, folle senēs.

author: Martial
text:
Haec rapit Antaeī vēlōx in pulvere draucus,
Grandia quī vānō colla labōre facit.

author: Martial
text:
Quid pereunt stultō fortēs haltēre lacertī?
Exercet melius vīnea fossa virōs.

author: Martial
text:
Nē lutet inmundum nitidōs cērōma capillōs,
Hāc poteris madidās condere pelle comās.

author: Martial
text:
Pergamon hās mīsit. Curvō dēstringere ferrō:
Nōn tam saepe teret lintea fullo tibī.

author: Martial
text:
Gestāvit modo fronte mē juvencus:
Vērum rhīnocerōta mē putābās.

author: Martial
text:
Nūper in Ausoniā dominī spectātus harēnā
Hic erit ille tibī, cui pila taurus erat.

author: Martial
text:
Sī quis plōrātor collō tibi vernula pendet,
Haec quatiat tenerā garrula sīstra manū.

author: Martial
text:
Prōficiēs nihil hōc, caedās licet usque, flagellō,
Sī tibi purpureō dē grege currit equus.

author: Martial
text:
Quid mēcum est tibi? mē puella sūmat:
Ēmptōs nōn soleō polīre dentēs.

author: Martial
text:
Quod nec Vergilius nec carmine dīcit Homērus,
Hoc ex unguentō cōnstat et ex balanō.

author: Martial
text:
Rūsticus es? nescīs, quid Graecō nōmine dīcar:
Spūma vocor nitrī. Graecus es? aphronitrum.

author: Martial
text:
Balsama mē capiunt, haec sunt unguenta virōrum:
Dēliciās Cosmī vōs redolēte, nurūs.

author: Martial
text:
Grātum mūnus erit scissō nec inūtile ventrī,
Sī clārā Stephanī balnea lūce petēs.

author: Martial
text:
Dux lanterna viae clūsīs feror aurea flammīs,
Et tūta est gremiō parva lucerna meō.

author: Martial
text:
Cornea sī nōn sum, numquid sum fuscior? aut mē
Vēsīcam, contrā quī venit, esse putat?

author: Martial
text:
Ēbria nōs madidīs rumpit tībīcina buccīs:
Saepe duās pariter, saepe monaulon habet.

author: Martial
text:
Quid mē conpactam cērīs et harundine rīdēs?
Quae prīmum strūcta est fistula tālis erat.

author: Martial
text:
Dēfuerit sī forte puer soleāsque libēbit
Sūmere, prō puerō pēs erit ipse sibī.

author: Martial
text:
Taurīnō poterās pectus cōnstringere tergō:
Nam pellis mammās nōn capit ista tuās.

author: Martial
text:
Lambere quae turpēs prohibet tua prandia muscās,
Ālitis eximiae cauda superba fuit.

author: Martial
text:
Sordida sī flāvō fuerit tibi pulvere vestis,
Colligat hunc tenuī verbere cauda levis.

author: Martial
text:
Peccantis famulī pugnō nē percute dentēs:
Clāra Rhodos coptam quam tibi mīsit edat.

author: Martial
text:
Sī vīs esse satur, nostrum potes ēsse Priāpum:
Ipsa licet rōdās inguina, pūrus eris.

author: Martial
text:
Iste tibī faciet bona Sāturnālia porcus,
Inter spūmantēs īlice pāstus aprōs.

author: Martial
text:
Quī vēnit botulus mediae tibi tempore brūmae,
Sāturnī septem vēnerat ante diēs.

author: Martial
text:
Psittacus ā vōbīs aliōrum nōmina discam:
Hoc didicī per mē dīcere CAESAR HAVĒ.

author: Martial
text:
Corve salūtātor, quārē fellātor habēris?
In caput intrāvit mentula nūlla tuum.

author: Martial
text:
Flet Philomēla nefās incestī Tēreos, et quae
Mūta puella fuit, garrula fertur avis.

author: Martial
text:
Pīca loquāx certā dominum tē vōce salūtō:
Sī mē nōn videās, esse negābis avem.

author: Martial
text:
Sī tibi tālis erit, quālem dīlēcta Catullō
Lesbia plōrābat, hīc habitāre potest.

author: Martial
text:
Artis ebur medicae narthēcia cernis: habēbis
Mūnera, quae cuperet Paccius esse sua.

author: Martial
text:
Lūdite lascīvī, sed tantum lūdite, servī:
Haec signāta mihī quīnque diēbus erunt.

author: Martial
text:
Invīsae nimium puerīs grātaeque magistrīs,
Clāra Promēthēō mūnere ligna sumus.

author: Martial
text:
Nē mendīca ferat barbātī prandia nūdī
Dormiat et tetricō cum cane, pēra rogat.

author: Martial
text:
In pretiō scōpās testātur palma fuisse.
Ōtia sed scōpīs nunc analecta dedit.

author: Martial
text:
Dēfendet manus haec scapulās mordente molestō
Pūlice, vel sī quid pūlice sordidius.

author: Martial
text:
Nē toga barbātōs faciat vel paenula librōs,
Haec abiēs chartīs tempora longa dabit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōmina dat spondae pictīs pulcherrima pinnīs
Nunc Jūnōnis avis, sed prius Argus erat.

author: Martial
text:
Strāgula succīnctī vēnātor sūme verēdī:
Nam solet ā nūdō surgere fīcus equō.

author: Martial
text:
Accipe lūnātā scrīptum testūdine sigma.
Octo capit; veniat quisquis amīcus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Fēmineam nōbīs cherson sī crēdis inesse,
Dēciperis: pelagī mascula praeda sumus.

author: Martial
text:
Accipe fēlīcēs, Atlantica mūnera, silvās:
Aurea quī dederit dōna, minōra dabit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn sum crīspa quidem nec silvae fīlia Maurae,
Sed nōrunt lautās et mea ligna dapēs.

author: Martial
text:
Grandia taurōrum portant quī corpora, quaeris,
An Libycās possint sustinuisse trabēs?

author: Martial
text:
Pūncta notīs īlex et acūtā cuspide clūsa
Saepe redēmptōris prōdere fūrta solet.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn est ista recēns nec nostrī glōria caelī:
Prīmus in hīs Mentor, dum facit illa, bibit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōs sumus audācis plēbēja toreumata vitrī,
Nostra neque ārdentī gemma ferītur aquā.

author: Martial
text:
Quamvīs Callācō rubeam generōsa metallō,
Glōrior arte magis: nam Myos iste labor.

author: Martial
text:
Vīlia sūtōris calicem monimenta Vatīnī
Accipe; sed nāsus longior ille fuit.

author: Martial
text:
Grandia nē violā parvō chrȳsendeta mullō:
Ut minimum, lībrās dēbet habēre duās.

author: Martial
text:
Ārrētīna nimis nē spernās vāsa monēmus:
Lautus erat Tuscīs Porsena fictilibus.

author: Martial
text:
Barbara dē pictīs vēnī bascauda Britannīs;
Sed mē jam māvolt dīcere Rōma suam.

author: Martial
text:
Sī nōn ignōta est doctī tibi terra Catullī,
Pōtāstī testā Raetica vīna meā.

author: Martial
text:
Cum mihi bōlētī dederint tam nōbile nōmen,
Prōtotomīs, pudet heu! servio cōliculīs.

author: Martial
text:
Accipe nōn vīlī calicēs dē pulvere nātōs,
Sed Surrentīnae lēve toreuma rotae.

author: Martial
text:
Sētīnōs, moneō, nostrā nive frange trientēs:
Pauperiōre merō tingere līna potes.

author: Martial
text:
Attenuāre nivēs nōrunt et lintea nostra:
Frīgidior cōlō nōn salit unda tuō.

author: Martial
text:
Frīgida nōn dēerit, nōn dēerit calda petentī.
Sed tū mōrōsā lūdere parce sitī.

author: Martial
text:
Hic tibi dōnātur pānsā ruber urceus ānsā.
Stōicus hōc gelidam Fronto petēbat aquam.

author: Martial
text:
Nōs Satyrī, nōs Bacchus amat, nōs ēbria tigris,
Perfūsōs dominī lambere docta pedēs.

author: Martial
text:
Quae nōn sollicitus teneat servetque minister,
Sūme Saguntīnō pōcula facta lutō.

author: Martial
text:
Gemmātum Scythicīs ut lūceat ignibus aurum,
Aspice. Quot digitōs exuit iste calix!

author: Martial
text:
Hāc licet in gemmā, servat quae nōmina Cosmī,
Luxuriōse, bibās, sī foliāta sitīs.

author: Martial
text:
Frangere dum metuis, frangēs crystallina: peccant
Sēcūrae nimium sollicitaeque manūs.

author: Martial
text:
Ā Jove quī veniet, miscenda ad pōcula largās
Fundet nimbus aquās: hic tibi vīna dabit.

author: Martial
text:
Sī caldum pōtās, ārdentī murra Falernō
Convenit et melior fit sapor inde merō.

author: Martial
text:
Hanc tibi Cūmānō rubicundam pulvere testam
Mūnicipem mīsit casta Sibylla suam.

author: Martial
text:
Aspicis ingenium Nīlī: quibus addere plūra
Dum cupit, āh quotiēns perdidit auctor opus!

author: Martial
text:
Spolētīna bibis vel Marsīs condita cellīs:
Quō tibi dēcoctae nōbile frīgus aquae?

author: Martial
text:
Nōn pōtāre nivem, sed aquam pōtāre recentem
Dē nive commenta est ingeniōsa sitis.

author: Martial
text:
Massiliae fūmōs miscēre nivālibus undīs
Parce, puer, cōnstet nē tibi plūris aqua.

author: Martial
text:
Dum poscor crepitū digitōrum et verna morātur,
Ō quotiēns paelex culcita facta mea est!

author: Martial
text:
Quamvīs mē ligulam dīcant equitēsque patrēsque,
Dīcor ab indoctīs lingula grammaticīs.

author: Martial
text:
Sum cocleīs habilis, sed nec minus ūtilis ōvīs.
Numquid scīs, potius cūr cocleāre vocer?

author: Martial
text:
Ante frequēns, sed nunc rārus nōs dōnat amīcus.
Fēlīx, cui comes est nōn aliēnus eques.

author: Martial
text:
Saepe gravis digitīs ēlābitur ānulus ūnctīs,
Tūta meā fīet sed tua gemma fidē.

author: Martial
text:
Rōmānōs rērum dominōs gentemque togātam
Ille facit, magnō quī dedit astra patrī.

author: Martial
text:
Sī mātūtīnōs facile est tibi perdere somnōs,
Attrītā veniet sportula saepe togā.

author: Martial
text:
Pauperis est mūnus, sed nōn est pauperis ūsus:
Hanc tibi prō laenā mittimus endromida.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi turbātō Canusīna simillima mulsō
Mūnus erit. Gaudē: nōn cito fīet anus.

author: Martial
text:
Gallia Santonicō vestit tē bardocucullō.
Cercopithēcōrum paenula nūper erat.

author: Martial
text:
Rōma magis fuscīs vestītur, Gallia rūfīs,
Et placet hic puerīs mīlitibusque colōs.

author: Martial
text:
Ingrediāre viam caelō licet usque serēnō,
Ad subitās numquam scortea dēsit aquās.

author: Martial
text:
Sī venetō prasinōve favēs, quid coccina sūmēs?
Nē fīās istā trānsfuga sorte, vidē.

author: Martial
text:
Sī possem, tōtās cuperem mīsisse lacernās:
Nunc tantum capitī mūnera mitto tuō.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn est lāna mihi mendāx, nec mūtor ahēnō.
Sīc placeant Tyriae: mē mea tīnxit ovis.

author: Martial
text:
Fascia, crēscentēs dominae compesce papillās,
Ut sit quod capiat nostra tegatque manus.

author: Martial
text:
Amphitheātrālī nōs commendāmus ab ūsū,
Cum teget algentēs alba lacerna togās.

author: Martial
text:
Nec fora sunt nōbīs nec sunt vadimōnia nōta:
Hoc opus est, pictīs accubuisse torīs.

author: Martial
text:
Sī recitātūrus dederō tibi forte libellum,
Hoc fōcāle tuās adserat auriculās.

author: Martial
text:
Tempore brūmālī nōn multum lēvia prōsunt:
Calfaciunt villī pallia vestra meī.

author: Martial
text:
Nōbilius villōsa tegant tibi lintea citrum:
Orbibus in nostrīs circulus esse potest.

author: Martial
text:
Jungere nescīstī nōbīs, ō stulte, lacernās:
Induerās albās, exue callainās.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn hōs lāna dedit, sed olentis barba marītī:
Cīnyphiō poterit planta latēre sinū.

author: Martial
text:
Dum toga per quīnās gaudet requiēscere lūcēs,
Hōs poteris cultūs sūmere jūre tuō.

author: Martial
text:
Vellera cōnsūmunt Patavīnae multa trilīcēs,
Et pinguēs tunicās serra secāre potest.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi sorte datur tergendīs spongia mēnsīs
Ūtilis, expressō cum levis imbre tumet.

author: Martial
text:
Is mihi candor inest, villōrum grātia tanta,
Ut mē vel mediā sūmere messe velīs.

author: Martial
text:
Tingue caput Cosmī foliō, cervīcal olēbit:
Perdidit unguentum cum coma, plūma tenet.

author: Martial
text:
Strāgula purpureīs lūcent villōsa tapētīs.
Quid prōdest, sī tē congelat uxor anus?

author: Martial
text:
Nūdō strāgula nē torō patērent,
Jūnctae nōs tibi vēnimus sorōrēs.

author: Martial
text:
Mammōsās metuō; tenerae mē trāde puellae,
Ut possint niveō pectore līna fruī.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi Memphītis tellūs dat mūnera: victa est
Pectine Nīliacō jam Babylonos acus.

author: Martial
text:
Longa satis nunc sum; dulcī sed pondere venter
Sī tumeat, fīam tunc tibi zōna brevis.

author: Martial
text:
Lōdīcēs mittet doctī tibi terra Catullī:
Nōs Helicāoniā dē regiōne sumus.

author: Martial
text:
Det tunicam locuplēs: ego tē praecingere possum.
Essem sī locuplēs, mūnus utrumque darem.

author: Martial
text:
Ēbria Sīdoniae cum sim dē sanguine conchae,
Nōn videō, quārē sōbria lāna vocer.

author: Martial
text:
Velleribus prīmīs Āpūlia, Parma secundīs
Nōbilis; Altīnum tertia laudat ovis.

author: Martial
text:
Nōs Lacedaemoniae pāstor dōnāvit amīcae:
Dēterior Lēdae purpura mātris erat.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn tantum pullō lūgentēs vellere lānās,
Sed solet et calicēs haec dare terra suōs.

author: Martial
text:
Lāna quidem trīstis, sed tōnsīs nāta ministrīs,
Quālēs nōn prīmō dē grege mēnsa citat.

author: Martial
text:
Oppressae nimium vīcīna est fascia plūmae?
Vellera Leuconicīs accipe rāsa sagīs.

author: Martial
text:
Tōmentum concīsa palūs Circēnse vocātur.
Haec prō Leuconicō strāmina pauper emit.

author: Martial
text:
Lassus Amȳclaeā poteris requiēscere plūmā,
Interior cycnī quam tibi lāna dedit.

author: Martial
text:
Fraudātā tumeat fragilis tibi culcita mūlā.
Nōn venit ad dūrōs pallida cūra torōs.

author: Martial
text:
Redde pilam: sonat aes thermārum. Lūdere pergis?
Virgine vīs sōlā lōtus abīre domum.

author: Martial
text:
Splendida cum volitant Spartānī pondera discī,
Este procul, puerī: sit semel ille nocēns.

author: Martial
text:
Reddidit Eurydicēn vātī: sed perdidit ipse,
Dum sibi nōn crēdit nec patienter amat.

author: Martial
text:
Dē Pompejānō saepe est ējecta theātrō
Quae dūxit silvās dētinuitque ferās.

author: Martial
text:
Fervida nē trītō tibi pollice pūsula surgat,
Exōrnent docilem candida plēctra lyram.

author: Martial
text:
Indūcenda rota est: dās nōbīs ūtile mūnus:
Iste trochus puerīs, at mihi canthus erit.

author: Martial
text:
Garrulus in laxō cūr ānulus orbe vagātur?
Cēdat ut argūtīs obvia turba trochīs.

author: Martial
text:
Haec illī sine sorte datur, cui nōmina Rhēnus
Vēra dedit. Deciēns adde Falerna, puer.

author: Martial
text:
Glōria tam parvī nōn est obscūra sigillī:
Istīus puerī Brūtus amātor erat.

author: Martial
text:
Ad tē reptantī, puer īnsidiōse, lacertae
Parce: cupit digitīs illa perīre tuīs.

author: Martial
text:
Flectit ab invīsō morientia lūmina discō
Oebalius, Phoebī culpa dolorque, puer.

author: Martial
text:
Masculus intrāvit fontīs, ēmersit utrumque:
Pars est ūna patris, cētera mātris habet.

author: Martial
text:
Cūr ā tē pretium Danaē, rēgnātor Olympī,
Accēpit, grātīs sī tibi Lēda dedit?

author: Martial
text:
Sum figulī lūsūs: russī persōna Batāvī.
Quae tū dērīdēs, haec timet ōra puer.

author: Martial
text:
Ēlīdit geminōs īnfāns nec respicit anguīs.
Jam poterat tenerās hȳdra timēre manūs.

author: Martial
text:
Sum fragilis: sed tū, moneō, nē sperne sigillum:
Nōn pudet Alcīdēn nōmen habēre meum.

author: Martial
text:
Dīc mihi, virgo ferōx, cum sit tibi cassis et hasta,
Quārē nōn habeās aegida. ‘Caesar habet.'

author: Martial
text:
Mūtārī melius taurō, pater optime dīvum,
Tunc poterās, Īō cum tibi vacca fuit.

author: Martial
text:
Clāmābat tumidīs audāx Lēandros in undīs:
‘Mergite mē, flūctūs, cum reditūrus erō.'

author: Martial
text:
Ēbrius haec fēcit terrīs, puto, mōnstra Promētheus:
Sāturnāliciō lūsit et ipse lutō.

author: Martial
text:
Perlege Maeoniō cantātās carmine rānās
Et frontem nūgīs solvere disce meīs.

author: Martial
text:
Īlias et Priamī rēgnīs inimīcus Ulixēs
Multiplicī pariter condita pelle latent.

author: Martial
text:
Accipe fācundī Culicem, studiōse, Marōnis,
Nē nucibus positīs ARMA VIRUMQUE legās.

author: Martial
text:
Quam brevis inmēnsum cēpit membrāna Marōnem!
Ipsīus vultūs prīma tabella gerit.

author: Martial
text:
Hāc prīmum juvenum lascīvōs lūsit amōrēs;
Nec Glycerā puerī, Thāis amīca fuit.

author: Martial
text:
Sī comes ista tibī fuerit membrāna, putātō
Carpere tē longās cum Cicerōne viās.

author: Martial
text:
Cynthia, fācundī carmen juvenāle Propertī
Accēpit fāmam, nōn minus ipsa dedit.

author: Martial
text:
Pellibus exiguīs artātur Līvius ingēns,
Quem mea nōn tōtum bibliothēca capit.

author: Martial
text:
Hīc erit, ut perhibent doctōrum corda virōrum,
Prīmus Rōmānā Crīspus in historiā.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi, multiplicī quae strūcta est massa tabellā,
Carmina Nāsōnis quīnque decemque gerit.

author: Martial
text:
Ussit amātōrem Nemesis lascīva Tibullum,
In tōtā jūvit quem nihil esse domō.

author: Martial
text:
Sunt quīdam, quī mē dīcant nōn esse poētam:
Sed quī mē vēndit bybliopōla putat.

author: Martial
text:
Tantum magna suō dēbet Vērōna Catullō,
Quantum parva suō Mantua Vergiliō.

author: Martial
text:
Haec tibi quae fontēs et aquārum nōmina dīcit,
Ipsa suās melius charta natābat aquās.

author: Martial
text:
Hīs tibi dē mūlīs nōn est metuenda ruīna:
Altius in terrā paene sedēre solēs.

author: Martial
text:
Dēliciās parvae sī vīs audīre catellae,
Nārrantī brevis est pāgina tōta mihi.

author: Martial
text:
Hic brevis ad numerōs rapidum quī colligit unguem,
Vēnit ab auriferīs gentibus Astur equus.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn sibi, sed dominō vēnātur vertragus ācer,
Inlaesum leporem quī tibi dente feret.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn amo quod vincat, sed quod succumbere nōvit
Et didicit melius τὴν ἐπικλινοπάλην.

author: Martial
text:
Callidus ēmissās ēlūdere sīmius hastās,
Sī mihi cauda foret, cercopithēcus eram.

author: Martial
text:
Tam tremulum crīsat, tam blandum prūrit, ut ipsum
Masturbātōrem fēcerit Hippolytum.

author: Martial
text:
Aera Celaenaeōs lūgentia mātris amōrēs
Ēsuriēns Gallus vēndere saepe solet.

author: Martial
text:
Sit nōbīs aetāte puer, nōn pūmice lēvis,
Propter quem placeat nūlla puella mihī.

author: Martial
text:
Collō necte, puer, merōs amōrēs,
Ceston dē Veneris sinū calentem.

author: Martial
text:
Sūme Cythēriacō medicātum nectare ceston:
Ussit amātōrem balteus iste Jovem.

author: Martial
text:
Currant verba licet, manus est vēlōcior illīs:
Nōndum lingua suum, dextra perēgit opus.

author: Martial
text:
Lēvis ab aequoreā cortex Mareōtica conchā
Fīat: inoffēnsā curret harundo viā.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn mendāx stupor est, nec fingitur arte dolōsā.
Quisquis plūs jūstō nōn sapit, ille sapit.

author: Martial
text:
Mollia Phrixēī secuistī colla marītī.
Hoc meruit tunicam quī tibi, saeve, dedit?

author: Martial
text:
Sī sōlum spectēs hominis caput, Hectora crēdās:
Sī stantem videās, Astyanacta putēs.

author: Martial
text:
Haec, quae saepe solet vincī, quae vincere rārō,
Parma tibī, scūtum pūmiliōnis erit.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn erit in turbā quisquam Μισούμενος istā:
Sed poterit quīvīs esse δὶς ἐξαπατῶν.

author: Martial
text:
Dīc mihi simpliciter, cōmoedīs et citharoedīs,
Fībula, quid praestās? ‘Cārius ut futuant.'

author: Martial
text:
Nōn tantum calamīs, sed cantū fallitur āles,
Callida dum tacitā crēscit harundo manū.

author: Martial
text:
Praedo fuit volucrum: famulus nunc aucupis īdem
Dēcipit et captās nōn sibi maeret avēs.

author: Martial
text:
Dīc quotus et quantī cupiās cēnāre, nec ūnum
Addideris verbum: cēna parāta tibi est.

author: Martial
text:
Pauper causidicus nūllōs referentia nummōs
Carmina cum scrībās, accipe cor, quod habēs.

author: Martial
text:
Nōn satis est ars sōla cocō: servīre palātum
Nōlo: cocus dominī dēbet habēre gulam.

author: Martial
text:
Rāra tibī curvā crātīcula sūdet ofellā;
Spūmeus in longā cuspide fūmet aper.

author: Martial
text:
Mīlle tibī dulcēs operum manus ista figūrās
Extruet: huic ūnī parca labōrat apis.

author: Martial
text:
Surgite: jam vēndit puerīs jentācula pistor
Cristātaeque sonant undique lūcis avēs.
